fault_hypothesis: The list comprehension on line 75 uses an inclusive upper bound (`<= end`), but the specification requires an exclusive upper bound (`< end`). This causes points with timestamps exactly equal to `end` to be incorrectly included in the result.

constraints.intent: The timestamp of every returned point must be strictly less than the end of the range.

spec.expr: not any(p[0] == end for p in __cbfl_result)

constraints.intent: No returned point should have a timestamp exactly equal to the end boundary.

spec.expr: len(__cbfl_result) == sum(1 for p in points if start <= p[0] < end)

constraints.intent: The number of returned points must equal the count of input points satisfying the correct half-open interval condition.
